claim.text: 2024 IBC with Denver amendments per D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16
claim.context: Denver Building Code (DBC) | 2024 IBC with Denver amendments per D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16

verdicts.note: Official Denver government documents confirm that D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16 is the actual statutory section through which Denver adopts and amends the 2024 International Building Code and Fire Code.

claim.text: 2024 IFC with Denver amendments per D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16
claim.context: Denver Fire Code (DFC) | 2024 IFC with Denver amendments per D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16

verdicts.note: The claim is explicitly confirmed in Denver's official 2025 Denver Fire Code document, which states "The 2024 International Fire Code adopted in D.R.M.C. Sec. 10-16 is amended in the following particulars."

claim.context: Denver Plumbing Code (DPC) | 2024 IPC with Denver amendments
Denver Energy Code (DEC) | 2021 IECC with Denver amendments
Occupancy Classification | IBC 2024 §302 | Ground floor: M, Floors 2-5: R-2

verdicts.note: IBC 2024 §302 is a real code section, but Denver's official sources do not appear to reference it in publicly available online documents.

claim.context: Construction Type | IBC 2024 §602 | Type III-A specified
verify fire-resistance ratings per IBC Table 601
Height Limits - Stories | IBC 2024 Table 504.3 | Type III-A: R-2 = 4 stories max

verdicts.note: The claim incorrectly attributes story limits to IBC 2024 Table 504.3, which specifies height in feet; story limits are found in Table 504.4.

claim.context: Type III-A: R-2 = 4 stories max, M = 4 stories max. Project = 5 stories - EXCEEDS LIMIT

verdicts.note: The claim that Type III-A R-2 construction has a maximum of 4 stories is consistent with standard building code Table 504.4 limits for this construction type and occupancy classification.

claim.context: Height Limits - Feet | IBC 2024 Table 504.4 | Type III-A: R-2 = 65 ft max

verdicts.note: Table 504.4 governs number of stories, not height in feet; height limits in feet are specified in Table 504.3, not Table 504.4.

claim.text: Type III-A R-2 maximum 65 feet height
claim.context: Type III-A: R-2 = 65 ft max, M = 65 ft max. Project = 60 ft - COMPLIES if sprinklered

verdicts.note: The specific Table 503 height and area limits for Type III-A R-2 construction from Denver Building Code could not be located in available search results, despite multiple searches of official Denver building code documents.

claim.context: Allowable Area per Floor | IBC 2024 Table 506.2 | Type III-A: R-2 = 12,000 SF, M = 9,000 SF

claim.text: Type III-A R-2 allowable area 12,000 SF per floor
claim.context: Type III-A: R-2 = 12,000 SF, M = 9,000 SF

verdicts.note: The specific allowable area table for Type III-A construction with R-2 occupancy could not be located in accessible Denver building code documents through web search.

claim.text: Type III-A M allowable area 9,000 SF per floor
claim.context: Type III-A: R-2 = 12,000 SF, M = 9,000 SF

verdicts.note: While Denver's building code documents were located, the actual allowable area table (Table 506.2) containing the specific numerical values for Type III-A construction types and occupancies could not be accessed through the available search results.

claim.context: Sprinkler Requirement | IBC 2024 §903.2 | Required for 5-story building

verdicts.note: While IBC 2024 §903.2 is a real section in the International Building Code covering sprinkler requirements, the claim cannot be verified against Denver's official adopted building code documents, which were not accessible in the search results.

claim.text: Automatic sprinkler system required for 5-story building and mixed occupancy
claim.context: Sprinkler Requirement | IBC 2024 §903.2 | Required for 5-story building and mixed occupancy - REQUIRED

verdicts.note: The claim misrepresents IBC 2024 §903.2 by suggesting a blanket sprinkler requirement for "5-story building and mixed occupancy," when actual code requirements are occupancy-specific and condition-dependent, not based on building height and mixed-use status alone.

claim.context: Occupancy Separation | IBC 2024 Table 508.4 | R-2/M separation requirements
Seismic Design | Denver Building Code §1613.2.5.3 | Seismic Design Category B minimum

verdicts.note: The exact section citation §1613.2.5.3 is confirmed on authoritative sources and correctly describes the Seismic Design Category B minimum requirement for Denver.

claim.text: Denver requires Seismic Design Category B minimum
claim.context: Seismic Design | Denver Building Code §1613.2.5.3 | Seismic Design Category B minimum - APPLIES

verdicts.note: The exact claim is confirmed on authoritative sources; Section 1613.2.5.3 of Denver Building Code explicitly requires Seismic Design Category B as a minimum for all buildings and structures.

claim.context: Occupant Load - Retail | IBC 2024 Table 1004.5 | 8,000 SF ÷ 30 = 267 occupants

verdicts.note: IBC 2024 Table 1004.5 exists and is the authoritative source for occupant load calculations; the retail factor of 30 gross sf/person is confirmed in current code references.

claim.context: Occupant Load - Retail | IBC 2024 Table 1004.5 | 8,000 SF ÷ 30 = 267 occupants

verdicts.note: The cited IBC 2024 Table 1004.5 retail occupant load factor of 30 gross SF/person is confirmed, and the mathematical calculation (8,000 ÷ 30 = 267) is correct.
